title: ZenHub vs Taiga for Solo users
meta_description: ZenHub vs Taiga for solo developers who want task boards integrated with code without self-hosting or server upkeep.
constraint_lens: You want development tasks integrated with your code without managing servers or maintaining separate hosting environments.

verdict.summary: ZenHub wins for solo users who want dev tasks integrated directly with their code repository. It runs as a hosted service connected to GitHub without managing your own server. Taiga often involves self-hosting or maintaining a separate instance. If self-hosting and server upkeep are required to keep the board running, Taiga fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If self-hosting and server upkeep are required to keep the board running, Taiga fails first.

sections.heading: Best fit for zero-maintenance dev boards
sections.content: You track development tasks alone and refuse to maintain separate hosting environments. ZenHub integrates directly into GitHub issues and pull requests inside the same interface. Taiga can require hosting a server or maintaining a dedicated instance apart from your code platform.

bullets.why_it_matters: You manage tasks directly alongside code without switching platforms.
You avoid server setup and version maintenance.

bullets.point: Board views tied to repository workflows
bullets.why_it_matters: Issues move across pipelines without syncing separate systems.
You can customize the platform if you manage hosting yourself.
You switch between agile frameworks within the same system.
You define task stages in detail, though configuration may require maintenance.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You require full open-source control and deep customization beyond repository integration.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Taiga if you are willing to manage hosting for full flexibility.

items.fails_when: You must maintain server updates or troubleshoot hosting issues to keep boards running.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to ZenHub for hosted integration with your code.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If you already maintain your own server environment and want full customization control, Taiga may feel manageable.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: If you want tasks integrated directly with GitHub and no server upkeep, choose ZenHub.
If self-hosting sounds like ongoing work, avoid Taiga.
If full open-source control matters more than maintenance load, consider Taiga.

faqs.a: It can be self-hosted, which means managing server setup and updates.
Yes. It connects directly to GitHub issues and pull requests.

faqs.q: Which is easier for a solo developer with no maintenance time?
faqs.a: ZenHub is easier because it avoids separate server management.
It is preferable if you want full open-source control and are comfortable hosting it.
